compiled_state.title: Sub-agents can introduce field name mismatches that tests miss when fixtures mirror the bug
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description
During epic dso-5ooy, a sub-agent implemented overlay-dispatch.sh reading
privacy_overlay instead of performance_overlay from classifier JSON. The
corresponding test fixtures also used privacy_overlay, so all 4 tests passed
despite the field name being wrong. The completion verifier caught the mismatch
by running a live functional test against the actual classifier output.

## Root cause
RED test writer and GREEN implementation agents independently chose the same
wrong field name. Test fixtures mirrored the implementation bug, creating a
closed loop where tests validated consistency with themselves rather than
correctness against the contract.

## Suggested fix
Explore adding a contract-compliance check to the RED test writer agent prompt:
when a test references a field name from another component's output, verify the
field name exists in the corresponding contract document before writing the test.
Alternatively, add a post-batch check that cross-references field names in test
fixtures against their source contracts.

## Incident reference
Epic dso-5ooy, task 67e2-2912 (overlay-dispatch.sh), fixed in commit 48a3364e.

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | LLM-behavioral: sub-agent field name mismatches. Fix requires editing agent/skill definitions (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral (dual-signal: ticket references agent field-name mismatch behavior + fix targets agent/skill definitions)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Investigation RESULT (bot-psychologist): ROOT_CAUSE: RED test writer Pre-Flight Checklist Section 5 has no step to verify cross-component field names against contract documents. Dispatch context only injects task description, not contract docs. Both RED test writer and GREEN implementer derive field names from task description text rather than authoritative contract. CONFIDENCE: high. FIX: Add contract lookup step to Pre-Flight Checklist and/or inject contract references in dispatch context.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
